data.title: SunPower Inc. Delays 2025 Annual Filing
data.description:
SunPower Inc ( (SPWR) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SunPower Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to announce a delay in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 28, 2025. The company is postponing its Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report), covering its 2025 financial performance and operations.

Management says the delay stems from the need for additional time to complete year-end reporting procedures. SunPower notes it has devoted significant internal resources and is working diligently, suggesting process and workload pressures rather than a specific external shock or disclosed control failure.

The company expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the standard 15-day extension window allowed under SEC rules. It states that the report will be submitted as soon as practicable, indicating no expectation of a prolonged delay beyond that extension.

Preliminary, unaudited figures point to a sharp rise in revenue to $306.5 million in 2025 from $108.7 million in 2024, with higher costs and expenses but a narrower net loss of $36.2 million versus $56.5 million. These numbers, along with expectations for timely filing, are forward-looking and subject to change as final audit and review procedures conclude.

SunPower indicates it has kept current with all other required SEC filings over the past year, underscoring ongoing compliance efforts despite this delay. The notification is signed by Chief Financial Officer Wendell Laidley on March 30, 2026, signaling executive-level oversight of both the late filing and the company’s remedial actions.

The most recent analyst rating on (SPWR) stock is a Buy with a $5.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SunPower Inc stock, see the SPWR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SPWR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SPWR is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial quality—negative equity, meaningful debt, and negative operating/free cash flow—despite improving TTM profitability. Offsetting factors include constructive earnings-call momentum (record revenue/profit with guidance for continued operating profitability) and a relatively low P/E, while technicals appear mostly neutral rather than strongly bullish.

To see Spark’s full report on SPWR stock, click here.

data.title: Gulf Resources Delays 2025 Annual 10-K Filing
data.description:
Gulf Resources ( (GURE) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Gulf Resources, Inc. has notified regulators that it will delay filing its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The late notice was made via Form 12b-25, covering the company’s annual disclosure obligations for this period.

Management says the delay stems from needing extra time to finalize the 2025 financial statements and related footnote disclosures. The company indicates that completing these items by the original deadline would have required unreasonable effort or expense.

Gulf Resources, Inc. states that it is working diligently to complete the outstanding work on the Form 10-K (Yearly Report). The company expects to file the delayed annual report within the 15-calendar-day extension allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25.

The company does not currently anticipate any significant change in its results of operations versus the prior fiscal year in the earnings statements to be included in the Form 10-K (Yearly Report). End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Creative Realities, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors that it will delay its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company says the delayed filing relates specifically to its Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report), not to other periodic reports, which remain current.

Management attributes the delay mainly to heavy post-deal workload tied to several late-2025 transactions. These include the acquisition of the CDM Business, a preferred stock private placement, and a refinancing of its credit facilities, all of which have consumed substantial internal resources.

The integration of the CDM Business and the complexity of the financing moves have slowed the company’s ability to deliver information needed for the year-end audit. They have also delayed completion of disclosure controls and the evaluation of internal control over financial reporting as of December 31, 2025.

Creative Realities expects to file its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the standard 15-day extension window allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25. For investors, this signals a short, defined delay rather than an open-ended timing issue, though the company cautions that timing remains subject to execution risks.

The company warns that 2025 results will show significant changes versus 2024 because they will include two months of CDM Business operations and the impact of the financing transactions. However, it says it cannot yet provide reasonable quantitative estimates of these changes, framing all timing and impact comments as forward-looking statements subject to uncertainty.

Creative Realities notes that all other required filings over the past 12 months are up to date, indicating ongoing baseline compliance with SEC reporting rules. The notification is signed on March 30, 2026, by Chief Financial Officer Tamra Koshewa, underscoring management’s formal commitment to completing the delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) and addressing the remaining audit and control work.
